headline: A US government export-control order forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the week.
body: The shutdown of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 three days after launch, attributed to an export-control directive and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability, drove government regulation and frontier AI to the top of the rotation, with benchmark comparisons against Opus 4.8 and reports of an EU access ban accompanying the coverage. In parallel, the speed race intensified: Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion techniques to text and exceeds 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting local inference from memory-bound to compute-bound, while Xiaomi and TileRT's MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed reaches similar throughput via FP4 quantization, speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernels. System architecture broke out alongside new entrants Model Context Protocol, JSON-RPC, and LPU. Competitive positioning surfaced in claims that OpenAI is losing leverage as Microsoft, Apple, and Google promote their own models ahead of a GPT-6 decision.
